stem: Let U = {1, 2, ..., n}, where n is a large positive integer greater than 1000. Let k be a positive integer less than n. Let A be a set of exactly k elements from U. Let B = U \ A (the complement of A in U). We say a permutation of U separates A from B if all members of A appear before any member of B, OR all members of B appear before any member of A. How many permutations of U separate A from B?
reason: Stem is truncated (missing end of sentence and the question). Option A text incorrectly contains leftover question text 'How many permutations of U separate A from B? A. k!(n-k)!' instead of just 'k!(n-k)!'.
sourceAnswer is 'A. C(2n,n) / 4^n' indicating only option A is correct, but parsed correctOptions incorrectly includes ['A', 'C'].
